display.purpose: Page title, set beside the logo mark. 800 weight, -0.02em tracking.

body.purpose: Session descriptions. 13px/1.5, bumped up from 12px for readability.

label.purpose: Badges, pills, week numbers, dates. 700 weight so it holds up at small sizes on solid fills.

components.name: Monogram Badge (small)
components.description: 24px solid-color circle with a bold white initial; sits inline in the tab bar. Replaces the illustrated zodiac-animal icon set.

components.name: Monogram Badge (large, inverted)
components.description: 56px white circle with an identity-colored initial; used inside the solid-fill runner header card, where inversion (white circle vs. white text elsewhere) keeps it legible.

components.name: Active Tab
components.description: Selected runner tab: solid identity-color pill fill, white text, inverted badge.
components.css: .ds-tab{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;padding:5px 16px 5px 5px;border-radius:999px;border:1.5px solid oklch(0.88 0.008 132);font-size:13px;font-weight:600;cursor:pointer;font-family:inherit;transition:background .2s cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1),color .2s cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1)} .ds-tab-active{background:oklch(0.44 0.19 232);border-color:oklch(0.44 0.19 232);color:#fff} .ds-badge-inv{background:#fff;color:oklch(0.44 0.19 232)} .ds-tab:focus-visible{outline:2px solid oklch(0.52 0.18 132);outline-offset:2px}

components.name: Runner Header Card
components.description: Solid gradient-fill card (identity color to its deep variant), white text, large inverted badge. Replaces the previous pale-tint bordered card.

components.name: Key Race Pill
components.description: Solid-fill badge marking a key race in the tag legend, race calendar, and week badge.

components.name: Session Type Pill
components.description: Small solid-fill pill naming a session's type (Speed, Hills, Trail Long, etc). Replaces the previous colored border-left stripe on session cards — an absolute-ban anti-pattern.

components.name: Week Row
components.description: Collapsed week list item; expands in place on click. The week badge is a solid category-color pill with white text.
components.html: W07RaceLong run 24km

narrative.overview: This is the second pass on the same idea: a trailhead at first light, before a long climb. The first pass applied the concept as a light tint-swap and it read as barely different from the original beige dashboard. This version applies it with real conviction: solid saturated color fills carry the runner headers, the active tab, and every category pill, instead of pale washes. The page background stays pure white (light) / near-black (dark); restraint lives in the architecture, not in how boldly the brand colors get used once they appear. Each runner keeps a monogram badge instead of an illustrated mascot, and a small mountain-peak logo mark now sits by the page title.
keyCharacteristics: Solid, saturated color fills on runner headers, the active tab, and every category pill
Pure white / near-black page background; boldness lives in the components, not the canvas
Monogram badges replace illustrated icons; a small logo mark anchors the title
No side-stripe borders anywhere — session type moved from a colored border to a solid pill
Flat by default; the header card's gradient fill is the one deliberate exception

rules.name: The Solid-Fill Rule
rules.body: Every category and identity color is a solid fill with white text, not a pale tint with colored text. A tint reads as decoration; a fill reads as a decision.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Every weight in this system comes from the same font stack. Hierarchy is built with size and weight, never a second typeface.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces never cast a shadow. The header card's gradient fill is the one deliberate exception to pure-flat, and it never appears on anything else.

dos: Do use solid, saturated fills for every identity and category color — white text on top, not tinted-background-plus-colored-text.
Do keep Bg pure white (light) / pure near-black (dark).
Do use the monogram badge system consistently; never reintroduce per-runner illustrated icons.
Do use a solid-fill pill for session type; never a colored border on any card.
donts: Don't revert to pale tint chips/badges — that was the exact complaint that triggered this redesign.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe anywhere.
Don't introduce a cream/sand/beige tinted background.
